threatgraph_indicators.description: A process appears to be using a Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) application in a suspicious manner. This may be an attempt to gather information, gain access, or drop other malicious payloads. Investigate the process tree and the detected process.
A process appears to be suspiciously executing code via Powershell. Investigate the command line and process tree.
User hashes from SAM hive accessed from signed executable with untrusted root authority
A process launched whose behavior is likely related to a potentially unwanted program (PUP). Review the file.
An executable with two file name extensions was written. Adversaries can masquerade malware with a seemingly safe file extension to evade detection. Review the executable.
A process appears to have performed a system call by bypassing UMPPC hooks.
An IIS process wrote a PE file. This could be indicative of malicious or suspicious webshell to ensure persistence on the machine.
An IIS process wrote a script file. This could be indicative of malicious or suspicious webshell activity meant to compromise the web server, used to provide malicious network access.

description: A process gathered information about the operating system or hardware. Adversaries can use this to identify system vulnerabilities. Review the process tree.
name: GenericSystemInfoDiscovery

threatgraph_indicators.description: A process accessed system network configuration settings. Adversaries can use this for lateral movement efforts. Review the process tree.
A process gathered information about the operating system or hardware. Adversaries can use this to identify system vulnerabilities. Review the process tree.

description: A process accessed system network configuration settings. Adversaries can use this for lateral movement efforts. Review the process tree.
name: GenericSystemNetworkConfigDiscovery

description: An IIS process wrote a PE file. This could be indicative of malicious or suspicious webshell to ensure persistence on the machine.
name: PeFileWrittenByIIS

description: A process appears to have performed a system call by bypassing UMPPC hooks.
name: UmppcBypassSuspected

description: An IIS process wrote a script file. This could be indicative of malicious or suspicious webshell activity meant to compromise the web server, used to provide malicious network access.
name: ScriptWrittenUnderIISTree

description: A process appears to be using a Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) application in a suspicious manner. This may be an attempt to gather information, gain access, or drop other malicious payloads. Investigate the process tree and the detected process.
name: SuspiciousRMMUsage

description: A process appears to be suspiciously executing code via Powershell. Investigate the command line and process tree.
name: GenericPowershellExecution

description: A process has accessed the Chrome SQL Login Data database and loaded a decryption module. This may be an attempt to steal Chrome passwords. Review the process tree.
name: ChromeLoginDataAccessed

description: A process launched whose behavior is likely related to a potentially unwanted program (PUP). Review the file.
name: PUP

description: An executable with two file name extensions was written. Adversaries can masquerade malware with a seemingly safe file extension to evade detection. Review the executable.
name: DoubleExtensionWritten

description: A process has opened a handle to a Falcon process with excessive permissions requested. If this is unexpected, review the process tree and surrounding events.
name: SensorHandleOpenedLow

description: A process appears to be suspiciously executing code via Powershell. Investigate the command line and process tree.
name: GenericPowershellExecution

description: A process appears to be using a Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) application in a suspicious manner. This may be an attempt to gather information, gain access, or drop other malicious payloads. Investigate the process tree and the detected process.
name: SuspiciousRMMUsage
